title: Prevent next --prompt from silently attaching new bug work to the wrong active task
acceptance: For a divergent prompt, `next --prompt` returns a blocking or escalation result with explicit reasoning instead of auto-attaching to the current task.
The result tells the operator whether to open/import a new card, repair the current task metadata, or continue the active task intentionally.
Existing same-task continuation flows still work for real follow-up prompts.
New helper modules introduced by this card stay under 600 lines and keep the routing policy inspectable.
outOfScope: Reworking the whole broker conflict model
Adding a free-form bypass that lets agents override divergence without evidence
nonGoals: Do not optimize for fewer prompts at the expense of task-boundary correctness
rollbackNotes: Revert if valid same-lane continuation prompts stop resolving to their intended active task.
notes:
- Prefer evidence-based divergence checks over keyword heuristics alone.
- Use current task frontmatter, declared scope, and active claim metadata as
  primary signals.
- This card is a root-cause treatment for bug-lane misattachment; it must not
  introduce validator downgrade mechanisms.
